title: todo_write content length cap is 2000 chars magic number with no constant
description:
## Severity
Trivial

## Dimension
Naming / Documentation & Intent

## Location
src/tools/todo.rs:89-95 (content length check)

## Violation
```rust
Some(c) if c.len() > 2000 => {
    return (format!("Todo {i} content exceeds maximum length of 2000 characters"), true);
}
```

The 2000 appears twice as a magic literal. CLAUDE.md (Naming section) requires constants to be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE. The value is meaningful (CC parity) but undocumented at the call site — a maintainer changing it would have to update two literals and risk drift.

Also note `c.len()` counts bytes, not characters. A todo item containing 1000 emoji (4 bytes each in UTF-8) would fail the check at 4000 bytes but only 1000 visible glyphs. The error message says "characters" — wrong unit.

## Mandated refactor
```rust
const TODO_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS: usize = 2000;
...
Some(c) if c.chars().count() > TODO_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS => {
    return (format!("Todo {i} content exceeds maximum length of {TODO_CONTENT_MAX_CHARS} characters (got {})", c.chars().count()), true);
}
```

Or document explicitly that the limit is bytes-not-chars and rename the constant `TODO_CONTENT_MAX_BYTES`.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
